name: Business Analytics
description: Introduces quantitative reasoning, spreadsheet modeling, and business decision analysis. Students use descriptive statistics, forecasting, and dashboard techniques to support managerial recommendations.

name: Strategic Management
description: Capstone course examining strategic analysis, industry positioning, and execution. Students integrate knowledge from prior business coursework through cases, simulations, and presentations.

name: Marketing Principles
description: Survey of modern marketing concepts including segmentation, targeting, pricing, promotion, and channel strategy. Emphasizes how firms create value for customers in digital and physical markets.

name: Financial Management
description: Explores time value of money, capital budgeting, risk, and financing decisions. Students analyze how financial tools support corporate decision making.

name: Principles of Accounting I
description: Introduces financial accounting concepts, the accounting cycle, and preparation of basic financial statements. Students interpret the impact of business events on reporting outcomes.

name: Principles of Accounting II
description: Covers managerial accounting topics such as cost behavior, budgeting, performance evaluation, and decision support. Focuses on accounting information used by internal decision makers.

name: Management Principles
description: Examines planning, organizing, leadership, motivation, and control in organizations. Students apply management frameworks to contemporary workplace scenarios.

name: Strategic Management
description: Studies competitive positioning, strategic analysis, and implementation in complex organizations. Students synthesize cross-functional knowledge in a senior-level management setting.

name: Introduction to Computer Science
description: Introduces computer science as a discipline including problem solving, basic programming logic, data representation, and algorithmic thinking. Designed for students exploring computing pathways.

name: Principles of Computer Programming I
description: Covers foundational programming in a high-level language with emphasis on control flow, functions, arrays, and structured design. Students complete lab and programming assignments weekly.

name: Data Structures
description: Studies abstract data types, linked structures, trees, graphs, recursion, and algorithmic analysis. Programming projects emphasize efficiency, correctness, and implementation tradeoffs.

name: Design & Analysis of Algorithms
description: Advanced study of algorithmic paradigms, complexity analysis, greedy methods, dynamic programming, and graph algorithms. Students evaluate correctness and performance rigorously.

name: College Algebra
description: Reviews functions, equations, inequalities, exponential models, and graph interpretation. Emphasizes algebraic fluency for later quantitative coursework.

name: Calculus I
description: Introduces limits, continuity, derivatives, and applications of differentiation. Students develop symbolic, graphical, and numerical perspectives on rate of change.

name: Calculus II
description: Continues the calculus sequence through integration techniques, sequences, series, and parametric equations. Focuses on theory-supported problem solving and persistence.

name: Discrete Mathematics
description: Covers logic, sets, combinatorics, recursion, proof methods, and graph theory. Designed to support computer science and mathematically rigorous reasoning.

name: English Composition I
description: Develops college-level writing through rhetorical awareness, drafting, revision, and source-supported argument. Students write in response to contemporary issues and academic prompts.

name: English Composition II
description: Builds on first-year composition through research-based writing, rhetorical analysis, and source integration. Students complete sustained academic inquiry projects.

name: Introductory Psychology
description: Survey of the major concepts, methods, and findings in psychology. Topics include cognition, behavior, development, personality, and mental processes.

name: Principles of Biology I
description: Introduces cell structure, genetics, evolution, and scientific inquiry in biological systems. Includes laboratory experiences supporting the lecture sequence.

name: Principles of Biology II
description: Continues biology through organismal structure, ecology, and biological diversity. Laboratory exercises reinforce interpretation of empirical evidence.

name: Chemistry I
description: Studies atomic structure, stoichiometry, bonding, thermochemistry, and chemical reactions. Students complete quantitative laboratory work alongside the lecture.

name: Survey of United States History I
description: Examines United States history from early settlement through Reconstruction. Students engage with major political, social, and economic transformations.

name: American Government
description: Introduces the institutions, processes, and constitutional foundations of the U.S. government. Emphasizes civic literacy and contemporary policy debates.

name: Principles of Microeconomics
description: Introduces supply and demand, consumer choice, market structures, and efficiency. Students analyze how incentives shape behavior in markets and institutions.

name: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: Explores inflation, unemployment, growth, fiscal policy, and monetary systems. Students interpret macroeconomic indicators and model-based reasoning.

name: Public Speaking
description: Develops confidence and competence in oral communication through speech preparation, delivery, and audience adaptation. Includes multiple speaking performances.

name: Introduction to Sociology
description: Introduces sociological concepts, methods, and perspectives for understanding institutions and social change. Students examine inequality, groups, and culture.

name: Introduction to Philosophy
description: Survey of major philosophical questions in knowledge, reality, ethics, and human reason. Students practice argument analysis and concise analytical writing.

name: Elementary Statistics
description: Introduces descriptive statistics, probability, estimation, hypothesis testing, and regression. Students apply statistical reasoning to business and social science problems.

name: Principles of Computer Programming II
description: Extends introductory programming through object-oriented design, recursion, files, data structures, and testing. Programming assignments emphasize reusable code and debugging skill.

name: Computer Organization & Programming
description: Studies low-level programming, memory, machine representation, and system architecture concepts. Students bridge high-level programming with machine-level behavior.

name: System-Level Programming
description: Examines processes, threads, memory management, file systems, and systems programming interfaces. Projects develop practical skill in debugging complex software behavior.

name: Precalculus
description: Prepares students for calculus through functions, trigonometry, and analytic reasoning. Emphasizes symbolic fluency and interpretation of multiple representations.

name: Human Anatomy and Physiology I
description: Introduces structure and function of the human body with emphasis on homeostasis and organ systems. Includes laboratory observations and applied biological reasoning.

name: Chemistry II
description: Continues general chemistry through kinetics, equilibrium, acids and bases, thermodynamics, and electrochemistry. Lecture and lab work reinforce quantitative reasoning.

name: Survey of United States History II
description: Examines United States history from Reconstruction to the present. Topics include industrialization, reform, conflict, and modern social transformation.

name: World History to 1500
description: Survey of world civilizations through approximately 1500 with attention to exchange, empire, and cultural development. Students compare historical experiences across regions.

name: Global Issues
description: Introduces major global challenges including governance, development, conflict, and international cooperation. Students analyze cross-border issues through comparative frameworks.

name: Intermediate Microeconomics
description: Builds formal models of consumer choice, production, market equilibrium, and welfare analysis. Students solve analytical problems and interpret model assumptions closely.

name: Interpersonal Communication
description: Examines communication in personal, professional, and group relationships. Students analyze conflict, listening, identity, and message strategies in everyday settings.

name: Social Inequality
description: Studies inequality across class, race, gender, and institutions using sociological theory and evidence. Students interpret social patterns through written and discussion-based work.

name: Critical Thinking
description: Develops informal logic, argument analysis, and habits of disciplined reasoning. Students evaluate claims, evidence, and persuasion across academic and public contexts.

name: Cognitive Psychology
description: Explores perception, memory, language, problem solving, and attention through experimental findings. Students connect psychological theory to everyday cognition.

name: World Literature
description: Introduces literary texts from multiple cultural traditions with attention to genre, theme, and historical context. Students practice comparative interpretation and analytical writing.

name: Digital Marketing Strategy
description: Analyzes digital campaign planning, content strategy, platform metrics, and brand performance. Students interpret audience behavior and design measurable campaigns.
